content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Contextual Counter-Bias Prompting: Real-Time Bias Reduction via Adversarial Persona Injection",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) frequently reproduce subtle social biases in open-ended text generation, even when prompted to be neutral. Existing prompt-based debiasing often relies on static templates or explicit bias cues, limiting generalization to unseen contexts.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in real-world decision-making and conversational settings, ensuring robust, context-adaptive bias mitigation is crucial for trust and inclusivity. Current prompting approaches fail to account for context-specific nuances and evolving social norms, necessitating more flexible, scalable solutions.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting method that dynamically augments user prompts with adversarial 'counter-bias personas,' sampled in real-time from a curated bank of synthetic, demographically diverse perspectives. For any input, the system injects 1-2 context-relevant persona sentences that subtly counteract inferred bias risks (e.g., \"As a member of a marginalized group...\"). The injected personas are optimized during inference using a lightweight adversarial search to maximize reduction of bias indicators in output, without requiring model retraining or explicit stereotype enumeration. This approach generalizes to open-domain tasks and operates fully at prompt time.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on benchmarks such as BOLD, StereoSet, and CrowS-Pairs, comparing against self-debiasing prompts, chain-of-thought, and DeCoT baselines. Metrics will include standard bias and stereotype scores, contextual coherence, and human preference ratings for fairness and informativeness. Ablations will test the effect of persona diversity and adversarial search depth."
}
